era_context: Early 6th Dynasty Egypt, Akkadian Empire in Mesopotamia, mature Harappan Indus Valley Civilization. This year falls within a period of relative stability in the major civilizations, though underlying political and social changes are occurring.

events.title: 6th Dynasty Egypt: Reign of Teti or Pepi I
events.description: The 6th Dynasty continues. This year is not marked by any documented specific event, but falls within a period of continued pyramid construction, trade, and military activity. The reign of Teti (c. 2345–2333 BCE) or Pepi I (c. 2333–2305 BCE) is characterized by the maintenance of Old Kingdom administrative structures, though provincial governors are gradually accumulating more power and autonomy.
key_figures: Teti or Pepi I (6th Dynasty pharaoh)
events.certainty_note: No specific events are documented for this year. The assignment of this year to the 6th Dynasty is based on conventional chronology, with uncertainty of ±10–20 years.

disconfirming_evidence: No specific events are documented for this year. The historical record is blank.
historiographic_note: This year is not documented in any surviving source. The narrative is entirely inferred from broader chronological frameworks and archaeological context. The reliability of assigning specific events or even a specific dynasty to this year is low.
